what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard signal item that records the operator's decision on the global adopt-claude-design decision, specifying the chosen option 'Bring it in + wire it to the channel' and stating that work gated on that decision may resume.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-f473429f, address board://item-f473429f, type signal, source claude_code, state raised, and title about the adopt-claude-design decision
author_session is operator; channel and thread are empty strings
A single link of kind attached_to points to decision://global/adopt-claude-design
created and updated timestamps are identical, with a one-entry history showing a null-to-raised transition filed by operator
The markdown body repeats the decision, chosen option, and explicit permission for gated work to RESUME
standouts: It is explicitly labeled as a decision.decided signal
The chosen option is named directly in the title and body
It functions as a resume-gate authorization for dependent work
It links back to the originating global decision resource
concerns: channel and thread fields are empty, so the noticeboard routing context is incomplete
created and updated timestamps being equal is normal for a new item but leaves no audit trail of later edits
The link kind attached_to is generic; a decision-specific relation might be clearer
notes: A raised noticeboard signal that the operator has decided the global adopt-claude-design option and that gated work can now resume.
questions: What specific work was gated on decision://global/adopt-claude-design?
Which lane or system is responsible for performing the actual resume step?
What does 'wire it to the channel' involve technically?
Why are channel and thread left empty for this signal?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record the decision outcome and authorize resumption of gated work, which matches the file content.
apparent_themes: Linking noticeboard items to global decisions
intention_signals: To broadcast that a global design adoption decision has been finalized with a specific option and to unblock downstream work that was waiting on that decision.
novelty: It frames a decision outcome as a noticeboard signal item and explicitly encodes the resume authorization rather than only recording the decision.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, linkable signal that records the chosen decision option and communicates that gated work may resume, supporting downstream coordination.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard signal item item-f473429f records the operator's decision to adopt claude-design with the option 'Bring it in + wire it to the channel' and authorizes resumption of work gated on decision://global/adopt-claude-design.
intention_for_embedding: To communicate the decided global design option and signal that dependent, gated work may resume.
